records.chapter: Visiting a Believer Socially and When He is Ill
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 151. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: the Messenger of Allah (a.s) said, May I inform you of the people amongst you that are the people of Paradise?' 'Indeed, O Messenger of Allah,' they replied. 'They are the Prophets,' he said, 'the truthful, the martyrs, the newborns and those who visit their brothers at far away places in the city for no reason except seeking the pleasure of Allah.'
records.arabicText: 151 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: ألا أخبركم برجالكم من أهل الجنة؟ قالوا: بلى يا رسول الله، قال: النبي، والصديق، والشهيد، والوليد، والرجل الذي يزور أخاه في ناحية المصر، لا يزوره إلا في الله عز وجل.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 151. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: the Messenger of Allah (a.s) said, May I inform you of the people amongst you that are the people of Paradise?' 'Indeed, O Messenger of Allah,' they replied. 'They are the Prophets,' he said, 'the truthful, the martyrs, the newborns and those who visit their brothers at far away places in the city for no reason except seeking the pleasure of Allah.'

records.chapter: Visiting a Believer Socially and When He is Ill
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 152. It has been related that Abu Hamzah said: I heard al-'Abd al-Salih (The righteous servant of Allah; namely, Imam al-Kaďim (a.s)) saying: One who visits his fellow believer for no reason other that seeking Allah's pleasure and reward and fulfilling the pledges to obey Him, Allah will appoint for him seventy thousand angels from the time he leaves his house to the time he returns, calling out to him, 'Rejoice and enjoy Paradise. You have reserved a place in it!'
records.arabicText: 152 - عن أبي حمزة ، قال: سمعت العبد الصالح يقول: من زار أخاه المؤمن لله، لا لغيره يطلب به ثواب الله عز وجل، وينتجز مواعيد الله تعالى وكل الله به سبعين ألف ملك من حين يخرج من منزله حتى يعود إليه ينادونه: ألا طبت و طابت لك الجنة، تبوأت من الجنة منزلا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 152. It has been related that Abu Hamzah said: I heard al-'Abd al-Salih (The righteous servant of Allah; namely, Imam al-Kaďim (a.s)) saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn: One who visits his fellow believer for no reason other that seeking Allah's pleasure and reward and fulfilling the pledges to obey Him, Allah will appoint for him seventy thousand angels from the time he leaves his house to the time he returns, calling out to him, 'Rejoice and enjoy Paradise. You have reserved a place in it!'

records.chapter: Visiting a Believer Socially and When He is Ill
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 153. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: When one visits his brother-in-faith, the Lord [M.G] says, 'O you who are visiting! Rejoice and enjoy Paradise.'
records.arabicText: 153 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: من زار أخاه المؤمن قال الرب جل جلاله: أيها الزائر، طبت وطابت لك الجنة.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 153. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: When one visits his brother-in-faith, the Lord [M.G] says, 'O you who are visiting! Rejoice and enjoy Paradise.'

records.chapter: Visiting a Believer Socially and When He is Ill
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 154. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: the Messenger of Allah (a.s) said: Any Muslim who visits a sick believer will be absorbed in mercy. When he sits with him (the sick believer), mercy will submerge him; and when he returns, he will be accompanied by seventy thousand angels until he enters his house, every one of them saying, 'Rejoice and enjoy Paradise.'
records.arabicText: 154 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه و آله وسلم: أيما مسلم عاد مريضا عن المؤمنين خاض رمال الرحمة، فإذا جلس إليه غمرته الرحمة، فإذا رجع إلى شيعه سبعون ألف ملك حتى يدخل إلى منزله، كلهم يقولون: ألا طبت وطابت لك الجنة.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 154. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: the Messenger of Allah (a.s) said: Any Muslim who visits a sick believer will be absorbed in mercy. When he sits with him (the sick believer), mercy will submerge him; and when he returns, he will be accompanied by seventy thousand angels until he enters his house, every one of them saying, 'Rejoice and enjoy Paradise.'

records.chapter: Visiting a Believer Socially and When He is Ill
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 155. It has been reported that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said: Allah has a Paradise that none shall enter except three kinds of people: one who judges concerning himself with justice, one who visits his fellow righteous believer and one who does good for his fellow believer for the sake of Allah.
records.arabicText: 155 - وعن أبي جعفر عليه السلام قال: إن لله عز وجل جنة لا يدخلها إلا ثلاثة: رجل حكم في نفسه بالحق، ورجل زار أخاه المؤمن في البر، ورجل أبر أخاه المؤمن في الله عز وجل.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 155. It has been reported that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Allah has a Paradise that none shall enter except three kinds of people: one who judges concerning himself with justice, one who visits his fellow righteous believer and one who does good for his fellow believer for the sake of Allah.

records.chapter: Visiting a Believer Socially and When He is Ill
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 156. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far and Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) both said: On the Day of Resurrection, when a believing servant comes before Allah, He will account him with an easy accounting but then reproach him saying, 'O believer, what stopped you from visiting Me when I was sick?' The believer shall reply, 'You are my Lord and I am Your servant; and You are ever-living and never afflicted with suffering or illness!' 'One who visits a believer in sickness has in fact visited Me,' shall say the Lord [M.G], 'Do you not know so-and-so?' 'Yes,' the believer shall reply, 'I know him.' Allah [M.G] shall then say, 'What stopped you from visiting him when he was sick? Had you visited him, you would have visited Me. Then you would have found Me ever-present at the time of your need. If you then would have asked me for any need, I would have fulfilled it for you and not turned you away from it.'
records.arabicText: 156 - وعن أبي جعفر وأبي عبد الله عليهما السلام، قالا: إذا كان يوم القيامة أوتي العبد المؤمن إلى الله عز وجل، فيحاسبه حسابا يسيرا، ثم يعاتبه، فيقول له: يا مؤمن ما منعك أن تعودني حيث مرضت؟ فيقول المؤمن: أنت ربي وأنا عبدك، أنت الحي الذي لا يصيبك ألم ولا نصب، فيقول الرب عز وجل: من عاد مؤمنا فقد عادني، ثم يقول الله عز وجل، هل تعرف فلان بن فلان؟ فيقول: نعم، فيقول له: ما منعك أن تعوده حيث مرض؟ أما لو عدته لعدتني، ثم لوجدتني عند سؤالك ، ثم لو سألتني حاجة لقضيتها لك، ثم لم أردك عنها.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 156. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far and Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) both said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: On the Day of Resurrection, when a believing servant comes before Allah, He will account him with an easy accounting but then reproach him saying, 'O believer, what stopped you from visiting Me when I was sick?' The believer shall reply, 'You are my Lord and I am Your servant; and You are ever-living and never afflicted with suffering or illness!' 'One who visits a believer in sickness has in fact visited Me,' shall say the Lord [M.G], 'Do you not know so-and-so?' 'Yes,' the believer shall reply, 'I know him.' Allah [M.G] shall then say, 'What stopped you from visiting him when he was sick? Had you visited him, you would have visited Me. Then you would have found Me ever-present at the time of your need. If you then would have asked me for any need, I would have fulfilled it for you and not turned you away from it.'

records.chapter: Visiting a Believer Socially and When He is Ill
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 157. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far said: An angel once passed by a man standing at the door of a house. He asked, 'Servant of Allah, what brings you here?' 'My brother lives here,' replied the man, 'I intend to greet him.' 'Is there any blood relation between you or has a need brought you to him?' asked the angel. 'No,' answered the man, 'we are neither related nor has a need brought me to him except our Islamic brotherhood and respect for him. I am acquainted to him and I greet him only for the sake of Allah; the Lord of the worlds.' The angel said, 'I am a messenger of Allah to you and He sends you greetings saying to you: It is in fact I that you have visited and it is I that you have approached; and for that, I have made Paradise mandatory for you, put off My wrath from you and protected you from the Fire (of Hell).
records.arabicText: 157 - وعن أبي جعفر عليه السلام: إن ملكا من الملائكة مر برجل قائم على باب دار، فقال له الملك: يا عبد الله ما يقيمك على باب هذه الدار؟ قال: أخ لي في بيتها أردت أن أسلم عليه، فقال الملك: هل بينك وبينه رحم ماسة أو نزعت بك إليه حاجة؟ قال: لا، ما بيني وبينه قرابة، ولا نزعني إليه حاجة، إلا اخوة الاسلام، وحرمته، فأنا أتعاهده، واسلم عليه في الله رب العالمين، قال له الملك: إني رسول الله إليك، وهو يقرئك السلام، ويقول لك: إنما إياي أردت، والي تعمدت، وقد أوجبت لك الجنة، وأعتقتك من غضبي، وأجرتك من النار.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 157. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: An angel once passed by a man standing at the door of a house. He asked, 'Servant of Allah, what brings you here?' 'My brother lives here,' replied the man, 'I intend to greet him.' 'Is there any blood relation between you or has a need brought you to him?' asked the angel. 'No,' answered the man, 'we are neither related nor has a need brought me to him except our Islamic brotherhood and respect for him. I am acquainted to him and I greet him only for the sake of Allah; the Lord of the worlds.' The angel said, 'I am a messenger of Allah to you and He sends you greetings saying to you: It is in fact I that you have visited and it is I that you have approached; and for that, I have made Paradise mandatory for you, put off My wrath from you and protected you from the Fire (of Hell).

records.chapter: Visiting a Believer Socially and When He is Ill
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 158. It has been reported that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said: Any believer who visits a believer has in fact visited Allah; and any believer who visits a sick believer will be immersed in mercy. When he sits with the sick believer, mercy will inundate him; and when he leaves, Allah [M.G] will appoint for him seventy thousand angels who seek forgiveness and mercy for him, saying, 'Rejoice and enjoy Paradise' until the same time the next day and he shall have a kharif in Paradise.' The narrator asked, 'May I be ransomed for you, what is a kharif?' 'Kharif is a corner in Paradise,' he replied, 'It would take a rider forty years to journey through.'
records.arabicText: 158 - وعن أبي جعفر عليه السلام قال: أيما مؤمن زار مؤمنا كان زائرا لله عز وجل. وأيما مؤمن عاد مؤمنا خاض الرحمة خوضا، فإذا جلس غمرته الرحمة، فإذا انصرف، وكل الله به سبعين ألف ملك يستغفرون له ويسترحمون عليه، ويقولون: طبت وطابت لك الجنة إلى تلك الساعة من الغد، وكان له خريف من الجنة. قال الراوي: وما الخريف؟ جعلت فداك، قال: زاوية في الجنة، يسير الراكب فيها أربعين عاما.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 158. It has been reported that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Any believer who visits a believer has in fact visited Allah; and any believer who visits a sick believer will be immersed in mercy. When he sits with the sick believer, mercy will inundate him; and when he leaves, Allah [M.G] will appoint for him seventy thousand angels who seek forgiveness and mercy for him, saying, 'Rejoice and enjoy Paradise' until the same time the next day and he shall have a kharif in Paradise.' The narrator asked, 'May I be ransomed for you, what is a kharif?' 'Kharif is a corner in Paradise,' he replied, 'It would take a rider forty years to journey through.'

records.chapter: The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 159. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said: Feeding four Muslims is equal to freeing a slave from the descendants of Prophet Ishmael.
records.arabicText: 159 - عن أبي جعفر عليه السلام أنه قال: شبع أربعة من المسلمين يعدل رقبة من ولد إسماعيل (ع).
records.thaqalaynSanad: 159. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Feeding four Muslims is equal to freeing a slave from the descendants of Prophet Ishmael.

records.chapter: The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 160. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: If two believers were to enter the house of a believer and he were to feed them to full, this would be better than freeing a slave.”
records.arabicText: 160 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: ما من مؤمن يدخل بيته مؤمنين يطعمهما ويشبعهما: إلا كان ذلك أفضل من عتق نسمة.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 160. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: If two believers were to enter the house of a believer and he were to feed them to full, this would be better than freeing a slave.”

records.chapter: The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 161. It has been reported that 'Ali ibn Al-Husayn (a.s) said: Whoever feeds a hungry believer, Allah will feed him with the fruits of Paradise; and whoever gives a drink to a thirsty believer, Allah will quench his thirst (on the Day of Judgment) with an exquisite drink of Paradise; and whoever clothes a believer, Allah will clothe him with a green robe of Paradise. According to another tradition, the Imam said: Whoever clothes a believer will remain under the security of Allah as long as a thread of the cloth remains.
records.arabicText: 161 - وعن علي بن الحسين عليهما السلام قال: من أطعم مؤمنا من جوع، أطعمه الله عز وجل من ثمار الجنة، ومن سقى مؤمنا من ظمأ، سقاه الله يوم القيامة من الرحيق المختوم، ومن كسى مؤمنا من العرى، كساه الله عز وجل من الثياب الخضر " وفي حديث آخر " قال: من كسا مؤمنا من عرى لم يزل في ضمان الله ما دام عليه سلك.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 161. It has been reported that 'Ali ibn Al-Husayn (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Whoever feeds a hungry believer, Allah will feed him with the fruits of Paradise; and whoever gives a drink to a thirsty believer, Allah will quench his thirst (on the Day of Judgment) with an exquisite drink of Paradise; and whoever clothes a believer, Allah will clothe him with a green robe of Paradise. According to another tradition, the Imam said: Whoever clothes a believer will remain under the security of Allah as long as a thread of the cloth remains.

records.chapter: The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 162. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: Whoever feeds a hungry believer, Allah will feed him with the fruits of Paradise; and any believer who quenches the thirst of another believer, Allah will quench his thirst with an exquisite drink of Paradise; and any believer who clothes another believer will remains under the guard and protection of Allah as long as a shred of it remains.
records.arabicText: 162 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: من أطعم مؤمنا من جوع أطعمه الله من ثمار الجنة، وأيما مؤمن سقى مؤمنا سقاه الله من الرحيق المختوم، وأيما مؤمن كسا مؤمنا من عرى لم يزل في ستر الله وحفظه ما بقيت منه خرقه.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 162. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Whoever feeds a hungry believer, Allah will feed him with the fruits of Paradise; and any believer who quenches the thirst of another believer, Allah will quench his thirst with an exquisite drink of Paradise; and any believer who clothes another believer will remains under the guard and protection of Allah as long as a shred of it remains.

records.chapter: The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 163. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) once said to one of his companions, “O Thabit! Can you not free a slave every day?' 'May Allah make you prosper,' he replied, 'I do not have the (financial) power to do it!' 'Are you not able,' asked the Imam (a.s), 'to entertain four Muslims for lunch or dinner?' 'I am able to do,' replied the companion. 'By Allah I swear,' said the Imam (a.s), 'that is equal to free a slave.'
records.arabicText: 163 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال لبعض أصحابه، يا ثابت، أما تستطيع أن تعتق كل يوم رقبة؟ أصلحك الله، ما أقوى على ذلك، قال: أما تقدر أن تغدي أو تعشي أربعة من المسلمين؟ قلت: أما هذا فاني أقوي عليه، قال: هو والله يعدل عتق رقبة.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 163. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) once said to one of his companions,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “O Thabit! Can you not free a slave every day?' 'May Allah make you prosper,' he replied, 'I do not have the (financial) power to do it!' 'Are you not able,' asked the Imam (a.s), 'to entertain four Muslims for lunch or dinner?' 'I am able to do,' replied the companion. 'By Allah I swear,' said the Imam (a.s), 'that is equal to free a slave.'

records.chapter: The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 164. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: Whoever clothes a believer will remain in the mercy of Allah [M.G] as long anything of that cloth remains; and one who gives him a sip of water, Allah will quench his thirst with an exquisite drink (of Paradise); and one who satiates his hunger, Allah will feed him with the fruits of Paradise.
records.arabicText: 164 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: من كسا مؤمنا ثوبا لم يزل في رحمة الله عز وجل ما بقي من الثوب شئ، ومن سقاه شربة من ماء، سقاه الله عز وجل من رحيق مختوم، ومن أشبع جوعته، أطعمه الله عز وجل من ثمار الجنة.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 164. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Whoever clothes a believer will remain in the mercy of Allah [M.G] as long anything of that cloth remains; and one who gives him a sip of water, Allah will quench his thirst with an exquisite drink (of Paradise); and one who satiates his hunger, Allah will feed him with the fruits of Paradise.

records.chapter: The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 165. It has been narrated that Amir al-Mu'minin (a.s) said: Feeding your brother a mouthful is more loveable to me than giving a dirham in charity; and giving him a dirham is more lovable to me than giving ten in charity; and giving him ten Dirhams is more loveable to me than freeing a slave.
records.arabicText: 165 - وعن أمير المؤمنين علي عليه السلام أنه قال: لان أطعم أخاك لقمة، أحب إلي من أن أتصدق بدرهم، ولان أعطيه درهما، أحب إلي من أن أتصدق بعشرة، ولان أعطيه عشرة، أحب إلي من أن أعتق رقبة.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 165. It has been narrated that Amir al-Mu'minin (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Feeding your brother a mouthful is more loveable to me than giving a dirham in charity; and giving him a dirham is more lovable to me than giving ten in charity; and giving him ten Dirhams is more loveable to me than freeing a slave.

records.chapter: The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 166. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: If any believer feeds another believer to full, Allah will feed him from the fruits of Paradise; and if he gives him a sip to drink, He will quench his thirst with an exquisite drink (of Paradise); and if he clothes him, Allah will clothe him with a green robe (of Paradise) and he will remain under the protection of Allah as long as a thread of that cloth remains.
records.arabicText: 166 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: ما من مؤمن يطعم مؤمنا شبعا، إلا أطعمه الله عز وجل من ثمار الجنة، ولا سقاه شربة إلا سقاه الله من الرحيق المختوم، ولا كساه ثوبا، إلا كساه الله عز وجل من الثياب الخضر، وكان في ضمان الله تعالى ما دام من ذلك الثوب سلك.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 166. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: If any believer feeds another believer to full, Allah will feed him from the fruits of Paradise; and if he gives him a sip to drink, He will quench his thirst with an exquisite drink (of Paradise); and if he clothes him, Allah will clothe him with a green robe (of Paradise) and he will remain under the protection of Allah as long as a thread of that cloth remains.

records.chapter: The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 167. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said: (Amongst) the qualities most loved by Allah are three: a Muslim who feeds a hungry Muslim, relieves him from a distress or settle his debt for him.
records.arabicText: 167 - وعن أبي جعفر عليه السلام قال: من أحب الخصال إلى الله عز وجل ثلاثة: مسلم أطعم مسلما من جوع، أو فك عنه كربة، أو قضى عنه دينا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 167. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: (Amongst) the qualities most loved by Allah are three: a Muslim who feeds a hungry Muslim, relieves him from a distress or settle his debt for him.

records.chapter: The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 168. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: The first gift that a believer is presented with in his grave is the forgiveness for those who accompanied his bier.
records.arabicText: 168 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: أول ما يتحف به المؤمن في قبره أن يغفر لمن تبع جنازته.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 168. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: The first gift that a believer is presented with in his grave is the forgiveness for those who accompanied his bier.

records.chapter: The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 169. Sudayr reported: Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) once asked me, 'What stops you from freeing a slave every day?' 'My financial status will not allow that,' I replied. 'Can you feed a Muslim every day (to equal that reward)?' the Imam (a.s) asked. 'Must that Muslim be well-to-do or poor?' I asked. 'Even the well-to-do sometimes long for food,' the Imam (a.s) replied.
records.arabicText: 169 - وعن سدير قال: قال أبو عبد الله عليه السلام، ما يمنعك أن تعتق كل يوم نسمة؟ قلت: لا يحتمل ذلك مالي، قال، فقال، تطعم كل يوم رجلا مسلما؟ فقلت: موسرا أو معسرا؟ قال: إن الموسر قد يشتهي الطعام.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Sudayr reported: Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) once asked me, 'What stops you from freeing a slave every day?' 'My financial status will not allow that,' I replied. 'Can you feed a Muslim every day (to equal that reward)?' the Imam (a.s) asked. 'Must that Muslim be well-to-do or poor?' I asked. 'Even the well-to-do sometimes long for food,' the Imam (a.s) replied.

records.chapter: The Reward of one who Feeds a Believer, Quenches his Thirst, Clothes him or Pays off his Debt
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 170. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said: Feeding a Muslim is equal to freeing a human being.
records.arabicText: 170 - وعن أبي جعفر عليه السلام أنه قال: إطعام مسلم يعدل عتق نسمة.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 170. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Feeding a Muslim is equal to freeing a human being.

records.chapter: What Allah (azwj) has Forbidden a Believer to do Against a Fellow Believer
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 171. It has been related that Zurarah said: I heard Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) saying: The closest stage to infidelity that one attains is that a person takes one to be his brother-in-faith then the latter keeps track of his mistakes and slips that he may use them against him someday.
records.arabicText: 171 - وعن زرارة قال: سمعت أبا عبد الله عليه السلام يقول: أقرب ما يكون العبد إلى الكفر أن يكون الرجل مواخيا للرجل على الدين، ثم يحفظ زلاته و عثراته ليضعه يوما ما.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 171. It has been related that Zurarah said: I heard Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn: The closest stage to infidelity that one attains is that a person takes one to be his brother-in-faith then the latter keeps track of his mistakes and slips that he may use them against him someday.

records.chapter: What Allah (azwj) has Forbidden a Believer to do Against a Fellow Believer
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 172. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: One who accuses a believing man or believing woman falsely will be raised on the Day of Judgment by Allah in a messy substance unless he unsays it. The reporter asked, 'What is this messy substance?' It is pus flowing from the genitals of the unchaste women,' the Imam (a.s) replied.
records.arabicText: 172 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: من بهت مؤمنا أو مؤمنة بما ليس فيه، بعثه الله عز وجل في طينة خبال، حتى يخرج مما قال قلت: وما طينة الخبال؟ قال: صديد يخرج من فروج المومسات.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 172. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: One who accuses a believing man or believing woman falsely will be raised on the Day of Judgment by Allah in a messy substance unless he unsays it. The reporter asked, 'What is this messy substance?' It is pus flowing from the genitals of the unchaste women,' the Imam (a.s) replied.

records.chapter: What Allah (azwj) has Forbidden a Believer to do Against a Fellow Believer
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 173. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: The Prophet (a.s) said: One who spreads an indecency is like the one who started it; and one who dishonors a believer of something will not die until he perpetrates it himself.
records.arabicText: 173 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: قال النبي صلى الله عليه و آله وسلم: من أذاع فاحشة كان كمبتدئها، ومن عير مؤمنا بشئ لم يمت حتى يركبه.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 173. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Prophet (a.s) said: One who spreads an indecency is like the one who started it; and one who dishonors a believer of something will not die until he perpetrates it himself.

records.chapter: What Allah (azwj) has Forbidden a Believer to do Against a Fellow Believer
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 174. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: Between every two believers there is a covering (that binds them); if one of them says to the other, 'you are not a friend to me' then he commits apostasy; and if he accuses him of something, faith will dissolve in his heart like salt in water.
records.arabicText: 174 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: ما من مؤمنين إلا وبينهما حجاب، فإن قال له، لست لي بولي فقد كفر، فإن اتهمه فقد انماث الايمان في قلبه، كما ينماث الملح في الماء.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 174. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Between every two believers there is a covering (that binds them); if one of them says to the other, 'you are not a friend to me' then he commits apostasy; and if he accuses him of something, faith will dissolve in his heart like salt in water.

records.chapter: What Allah (azwj) has Forbidden a Believer to do Against a Fellow Believer
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 175. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: If a person says to his brother the lease expression of contempt, he cut off any relation between them; and if he says to him, 'you are my enemy,' one of them has then committed apostasy; and if he accuses him falsely, faith will dissolve in his heart like salt in water.
records.arabicText: 175 - وعن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام أنه قال: لو قال الرجل لأخيه أف لك انقطع ما بينهما، قال، فإذا قال له: أنت عدوي فقد كفر أحدهما، فإن اتهمه انماث الايمان في قلبه، كما ينماث الملح في الماء.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 175. It has been reported that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: If a person says to his brother the lease expression of contempt, he cut off any relation between them; and if he says to him, 'you are my enemy,' one of them has then committed apostasy; and if he accuses him falsely, faith will dissolve in his heart like salt in water.
